title: Market Brief — Friday, May 29, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. B200 util-avail is just 11% (Ornn), H200 at 14%, H100 at 16%, and even legacy A100 at only 20%, all below the 25% scarcity threshold. B200 spot surged 18.1% day-over-day to $8.34/hr, the biggest single-day move in the stack, while H100 jumped 12.7% to $4.13/hr. Demand is outrunning supply and pricing reflects it.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. B200 util-avail is just 11% (Ornn), H200 at 14%, H100 at 16%, and even legacy A100 at only 20%, all below the 25% scarcity threshold. B200 spot surged 18.1% day-over-day to $8.34/hr, the biggest single-day move in the stack, while H100 jumped 12.7% to $4.13/hr. Demand is outrunning supply and pricing reflects it.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), serving Llama 70B on H100 runs roughly $15.11/M tokens, meaning anyone pricing output at the medium tier around $3/M is selling at a loss of about $12/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are deep underwater on current-gen hardware. Grades: OpenAI Strong (premium pricing holds), Anthropic Neutral (Opus margin healthy, Haiku tight), Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when GPT-5.4 benchmarks comparably at $15/M, a 5.3x premium for incremental reasoning. Qwen3.5-397B at $3.60/M output on scarce B200s is undervalued given frontier-class parameter count, so buy Qwen inference capacity and short premium-tier reasoning model demand.
bullets: 11% util-avail on B200 (Ornn) signals severe shortage on Blackwell-generation compute.
16% util-avail on H100 (Ornn) keeps the workhorse GPU class firmly in scarcity territory.
$8.34/hr B200 spot median jumped 18.1% in a single day.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest frontier-adjacent model tracked across 51 endpoints.
193.3% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable GPU contract in the stack.
